summary: Gemma 4 is fully supported on the installed stack: mlx-vlm added the gemma4 vision architecture in v0.4.3 (Apr 2, 2026, day-0) and the installed v0.6.3 contains every important vision fix since — including the v0.6.3 fix for Gemma 4 unified silently dropping image inputs, so do not downgrade. mlx-community hosts the full checkpoint ladder (E2B/E4B/12B/26B-MoE/31B in bf16 through 4bit); the right vision candidates for an M4 Pro 24GB are gemma-4-e4b-it-4bit (~5.2GB) and gemma-4-12b-it-8bit — avoid OptiQ quants (vision towers dropped) and unverified E-series 4-bit quants (PLE quantization bug produced garbage output until recent re-uploads). No published evidence shows Gemma 4 beats Qwen2.5-VL-7B on jersey-digit OCR, so swap only after an A/B on labelled crops. SAM3 surprise: mlx-vlm itself already ships SAM 3 / SAM 3.1 support with mlx-community checkpoints (sam3-8bit at 1.04GB, sam3.1-bf16 at 3.49GB), with ~40ms/frame at 224px and ~190ms/frame at 1008px reported on M3 Max — both narrow uses (3000 crop mask-cleans + 3000 pitch-segmentation frames) extrapolate to well under an hour per match locally on an M4 Pro. The official facebookresearch/sam3 repo is CUDA-only (Triton, torch_generic_nms, hardcoded .cuda()) and does not run on MPS; the HF transformers implementation works on MPS with minor patches but is unnecessary given the MLX port. One flag: SAM3 weights are under Meta's custom SAM License, which violates the spike's declared Apache/MIT/BSD-only posture in requirements.txt; SAM2 (avbiswas/sam2-mlx, Apache-2.0) is the license-clean fallback for box-prompt mask cleaning.
gemma4_mlx: CHECKPOINTS (HF collection mlx-community/gemma-4): full ladder exists. Multimodal vision-capable repos: mlx-community/gemma-4-e2b-it-{bf16,8bit,6bit,5bit,4bit,mxfp8,mxfp4,nvfp4}, gemma-4-e4b-it-(same set; 4bit is ~5.22GB, vision included, converted with mlx-vlm 0.4.3), gemma-4-12b-it-{4bit,8bit,...} (image-text-to-text; 8bit is the most-downloaded at ~42k), gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-4bit (MoE), gemma-4-31b-it-{4bit...bf16}. CAVEAT 1 — OptiQ variants (gemma-4-12B-it-OptiQ-4bit, e4b-it-OptiQ-4bit, 31B-it-OptiQ-4bit) are TEXT-ONLY: the vision/audio towers were dropped during conversion, so they are unusable for jersey reading despite better text benchmarks. CAVEAT 2 — PLE bug: early mlx-community/unsloth 4-bit quants of the E-series (e2b/e4b) produced garbage output because Per-Layer Embedding layers were quantized (HF discussion mlx-community/gemma-4-e2b-4bit#1); a conversion patch landed and PLE-safe re-uploads exist (third-party, e.g. FakeRockert543), bf16/8bit unaffected; the 12B dense model has no PLE. Sanity-check any E-series 4bit checkpoint before trusting it. MLX-VLM SUPPORT: gemma4 architecture (vision, audio, MoE; SigLIP2 encoder, aspect-ratio-preserving) added in v0.4.3 (Apr 2, 2026). Vision-relevant fixes since: v0.4.4 'fix Gemma 4 vision + text degradation' + chunked prefill; v0.5.0 video; v0.6.1 unified+MTP; v0.6.2 long-text prefill; v0.6.3 (Jun 10, 2026, latest = installed version) 'fix Gemma 4 unified silently dropping image/video inputs' and 'fix thinking enabled by default'. The installed 0.6.3 is the first version where image inputs to the unified models are reliable — do not downgrade, and explicitly disable thinking for crop reads. IMAGE TOKEN BUDGET: configurable per image at 70/140/280/560/1120 tokens; Datature's analysis says 1120 is needed for small-text OCR/document work, but tight player crops with large digits should work at 280–560, which directly cuts per-crop latency. SPEED: no rigorous MLX-specific M4 Pro numbers published. Community Ollama/GGUF Q4 figures: M4 Pro 24GB ~16–22 tok/s on the 26B MoE; E4B hits 18–22 tok/s on an M1 Pro so an M4 Pro will be well above that; 31B is bandwidth-bound (~5 tok/s on 300GB/s systems — M4 Pro is 273GB/s) and memory-marginal at 24GB — rule it out. mlx-vlm's vision-feature caching reports prompt TPS ~550–825 and an 11x multi-turn speedup on Gemma 4. For 10–20 output tokens per crop, E4B-it-4bit should comfortably beat the current 3.5s/crop; 12B-it-8bit (~13GB) fits but leaves less headroom.
sam3_apple_silicon: MLX PORTS EXIST — two independent ones. (1) Best option: mlx-vlm itself added SAM 3 and then SAM 3.1 ('Add SAM 3.1 with Object Multiplex and optimized realtime pipeline', PR #880 in Blaizzy/mlx-vlm), with mlx-community checkpoints loadable via the same runtime already used for Qwen: mlx-community/sam3-8bit (1.04GB, ~860M params, text prompts + xyxy boxes + frame-by-frame video tracking) and mlx-community/sam3.1-bf16 (3.49GB, ~873M params, MultiplexMaskDecoder tracks 16 objects simultaneously, 2.4–4x faster tracking). Reported Apple Silicon speed (M3 Max, bf16): ~40ms/frame (25 FPS) at 224px, ~190ms/frame at 1008px tracking 5 objects. (2) Deekshith-Dade/mlx_sam3 + mlx-community/sam3-image (~3.5GB): image-mode only (text + box prompts), FastAPI/Next.js demo app, no published benchmarks, macOS 13+/Python 3.13+. OFFICIAL REPO ON MPS: facebookresearch/sam3 does NOT run on Apple Silicon — hard CUDA 12.6 requirement, Triton kernels, torch_generic_nms, hardcoded .cuda() in preprocessing (issue #164, open since Nov 2025, no maintainer response; HF discussion facebook/sam3#11 confirms M4 failure). Workaround per that discussion: the HuggingFace transformers implementation runs on MPS (install transformers from git, patch out a .pin_memory() call) and users confirm image and video inference work, but no M-series latency was reported — moot given the MLX port. PRACTICALITY ON M4 PRO 24GB: (a) mask-cleaning ~3000 crops/match — crops can run at 224–512px with a box prompt; extrapolating the M3 Max numbers, ~60–120ms/crop on M4 Pro ⇒ roughly 3–6 min/match, with only ~1–4GB memory (sam3-8bit), easily coexisting with the VLM in 24GB. (b) pitch segmentation on ~3000 frames at ~1008px — ~150–250ms/frame ⇒ roughly 8–13 min/match. Both jobs together are well under an hour per match. SAM2 MLX FALLBACK: avbiswas/sam2-mlx (GitHub) with avbiswas/sam2.1-hiera-small-mlx checkpoint — pure MLX (no PyTorch), point/box image segmentation + video tracking with corrections; also apple/coreml-sam2-large Core ML ports. SAM2 has no text prompts (fine for box-prompted crop mask-cleaning; not for text-prompted 'pitch' concept segmentation). LICENSE FLAG: SAM3/3.1 weights are under Meta's custom SAM License (permissive, commercial use allowed) — NOT Apache/MIT/BSD, so it breaches the explicit license posture in /Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/spike/video-analysis/requirements.txt ('every model/runtime here is Apache-2.0 / MIT / BSD'); SAM2 is Apache-2.0 and fits the policy as-is.
recommendation: GEMMA 4: trial it, don't blind-swap. The installed mlx-vlm 0.6.3 already supports it with all vision fixes (support landed in 0.4.3; the 'silently dropping image inputs' fix only landed in 0.6.3, so stay on 0.6.3+). Run an A/B against Qwen2.5-VL-7B-4bit on ~200 ground-truth jersey crops using mlx-community/gemma-4-e4b-it-4bit (5.2GB, speed candidate — but verify it's a PLE-safe re-upload by sanity-checking outputs first) and mlx-community/gemma-4-12b-it-8bit (quality candidate, ~13GB). Set the image token budget to 280–560 for tight crops and disable thinking. Swap only if digit accuracy is >= Qwen AND latency beats 3.5s/crop — there is no published OCR evidence that Gemma 4 wins at this size class, only that it's competitive on DocVQA/ChartQA at 31B, so the burden of proof is on the A/B. E4B will very likely win on speed; accuracy is the open question. Never use OptiQ quants for this (no vision towers) and skip 31B entirely on 24GB. SAM3: run it locally NOW, not deferred — but with a license caveat. The mlx-vlm SAM3 path (mlx-community/sam3-8bit, 1GB) makes both narrow uses near-free: ~3–6 min/match for 3000 crop mask-cleans and ~8–13 min/match for pitch segmentation, in the same runtime already installed, leaving the GPU job for genuinely heavy work. Deferring buys nothing. However, SAM3 weights are Meta SAM License, which violates the spike's declared Apache-only posture — either record a deliberate exception in requirements.txt, or use the Apache-2.0 SAM2 MLX port (avbiswas/sam2-mlx) for the box-prompted crop mask-cleaning and keep SAM3 only for the text-prompted pitch segmentation (or replace pitch segmentation with a keypoint/color method to stay fully Apache). Do not bother with the PyTorch-MPS transformers path; the official facebookresearch/sam3 repo will not run on this Mac at all.
